title: V1.0 backports
body:
Backports for v1.0.1

Bunch of cherry-picks off of master. Notably missing: anything related to the remote client work, any vendor changes aside from a small containers/selinux change.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/v1.0/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@baude @rhatdan @vrothberg @giuseppe Anyone care to give this an LGTM?

Needs a version bump.  v1.0.1?
I question backporting
`Add varlink support for prune `
We should not do anything for varlink at this point.
`podman image prune -- implement all flag `
We should not backport any new features

But not enought to block the process.

Image prune isn't a new feature so much as a major behavior change - it
matches us to the expected behavior of 'docker prune' instead of removing
far more images by default than people would expect.

The Varlink patch was mostly to convince the other prune patch to apply
cleanly without a complete rewrite.

On Sat, Feb 9, 2019, 07:21 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com wrote:

> Needs a version bump. v1.0.1?
> I question backporting
> Add varlink support for prune
> We should not do anything for varlink at this point.
> podman image prune -- implement all flag
> We should not backport any new features
>
> But not enought to block the process.

@vrothberg Since this is an 8-specific backport, I didn't pull in the apparmor stuff. Same reason on c/storage and c/image - we did a massive revendor after 1.0 and I didn't want to break anything

@rhatdan Now includes a version bump to 1.0.1

I'll do proper release notes for the main branch once this lands
Alright bumping build_each_commit timeout just for this branch - if we do more backports we'll probably hit it again
